ialize () throws IllegalActionException Description copied from interface: Executable Begin execution of the actor. This is invoked exactly once after the preinitialization phase. Since type resolution is done in the preinitiali

Since type resolution is done in the preinitialization phase, along with topology changes that may be requested by higher-order function actors, an actor can produce output data and schedule events in the initialize() method. Specified by: in

nts in the initialize() method. Specified by: initialize in interface Executable Overrides: initialize in class AtomicActor Throws: IllegalActionException - If a derived class throws it. postfire public boolean postfire () throws I

blic boolean postfire () throws IllegalActionException Description copied from interface: Executable This method should be invoked once per iteration, after the last invocation of fire() in that iteration. It may produce output dat

ire() in that iteration. It may produce output data. It returns true if the execution can proceed into the next iteration, false if the actor does not wish to be fired again. This method typically wraps up an iteration, which may involve upda

ly wraps up an iteration, which may involve updating local state. In an opaque, non-atomic entity, it may also transfer output data. The execution of this method is bounded. Specified by: postfire in interface Executable Overrides: postfire in cl
